ab: yu adidi’ henay i Takubuwan ku luma’ niyam, namicudad satu, malimad tayza i Taypak, hiza satu i tiza mauzip.
ch: 小時候我們家住在達固部灣，上學以後，就搬到了台北，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Takubuwan. After I started school, we moved to Taipei and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: yu micudad satu i tabakiay a cacudadan, mitucek kaku micudad tu nu Yuancumin lalangawan a cudad, makatineng tu Taywan Yuancumincu izaw ku canacanan yadahayay a lalangawan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: sisa, ayza a mihcaan i pipahanhanan nu lalud, taluma’ i niyazu’ kaku aci kakama mizizaw tu babalaki, maydih a makatineng tu nuuzipay a binacadan a lalangawan.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: waluay a bataan tu ku mihcaan niza babalaki, nika azihen mahiza henay u masakapahay.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: palitaan aku ciniza i cucuwaan ku katinengan niza a kamu, matinengay ciniza musakamu tu kamu nu Sakizaya, matineng musakamu aca tu kamu nu Pangcah, Ngayngay atu Lipun.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的撒奇萊雅語說得很流利，也會說阿美族語，客家話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Sakizaya, as well as Pangcah, Hakka and Japanese.

ab: palitaan aku ciniza kya matineng, alaw han yu adidi’ henay ciniza minanam tu tu kamu nu Sakizaya, acawa niza sa u Pangcah, i kakulian niza katuud ku katinengan tu Ngayngay a tademaw, kyu matineng ciniza musakamu tu cacanan a kamu.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學撒奇萊雅語，太太是阿美族，而他工作上認識許多客家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Pangcah, and he knows many Hakkas at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: yu pizizawan niyam sa, yadah ku nisakamuan nu babalaki tu sakay piadupay a laylay a tineng.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: kanahatu caay katalesem kami a mitengil, nika hanu limalay niyam a miluing, kya a satesek han tu a mitengil.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: yu adidi’ henay kaku caay ka tinetineng tu nu uzipay a binacadan a lalangawan, alaw han u Yuancumin ku uzip masikaz tu.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: yu yadah tu ku katinengan satu sa, manamuh ka u Sakizaya a tademaw, u masazumaay, u kasasenengan aca.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為撒奇萊雅族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Sakizaya is unique and something to be proud of.
